Title: AtPME17 is a functional Arabidopsis thaliana pectin methylesterase regulated by its PRO region that triggers PME activity in the resistance to Botrytis cinerea

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall strengthening through Ca2+-mediated egg-box formation; contributes to resistance against Botrytis cinerea
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf cell wall/apoplast at infection sites
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Homogalacturonan methylester removal in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pectin methylesterase family protein with N-terminal PRO region and C-terminal catalytic PME domain; signal peptide-containing type-I PME
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in leaves during Botrytis cinerea infection; also up-regulated by multiple pathogens and PAMPs, and by ABA, JA, SA, and ET-related immune signaling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest among six PME isoform genes induced in infected Arabidopsis leaves; induced at 48 h post inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; pathogen-associated molecular patterns elf18 and flg22; effector HrpZ; defence hormones ABA, JA, SA, ET; alkaline pH favors enzyme activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes methyl esters on homogalacturonan in a blockwise manner, generating contiguous de-methylesterified GalA residues that enable Ca2+-mediated egg-box formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the target modified pectin product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockdown/knockout mutants; RT-qPCR; PME activity gel diffusion assay; recombinant expression in Pichia pastoris; purified enzyme assay; pH and thermal profiling; CaCl2/ruthenium red precipitation assay for mode of action; proteolytic processing assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-induced PME activity, blockwise pectin demethylesterification, egg-box formation, and resistance to Botrytis cinerea; loss of function increases susceptibility and reduces PDF1.2 induction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Predominant contributor to pathogen-induced PME activity in this pathosystem; key control step for infection-induced pectin remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtPMEI1; putative AtPMEI4; putative AtSBT3.5; Pichia pastoris SUB2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious changes in vegetative rosette growth or major leaf cell wall neutral sugar/GalA composition in mutants under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public microarray/transcriptome meta-analysis; accession numbers not reported in the paper text provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate biomarker and engineering target for enhancing cell wall-mediated disease resistance; useful for biotechnology aimed at strengthening pectin structure under pathogen attack
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The N-terminal PRO region acts as an intramolecular inhibitor and must be proteolytically removed to activate the enzyme; AtPME17 activity is favored by alkaline pH, opposite to Botrytis cinerea PME activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPME17 is the most strongly induced PME isoform in Arabidopsis leaves infected by Botrytis cinerea. 2. pme17 mutants show reduced pathogen-induced PME activity and about doubled lesion area, demonstrating a positive role in resistance. 3. Recombinant AtPME17 is an active PME with alkaline pH optimum and blockwise demethylesterification mode. 4. AtPME17-generated de-methylesterified pectin forms Ca2+-mediated egg-box structures consistent with cell wall reinforcement. 5. The PRO region inhibits enzymatic activity until proteolytic processing occurs.
